actor_claims.quote_text: the Company receives an unsolicited alternative proposal from a third party

event_claims.actor_label: the Company and its affiliates and their respective representatives
event_claims.description: Under the merger agreement, the Company and its affiliates and their representatives may not solicit, initiate, encourage or facilitate alternative proposals or related inquiries, discussions or offers.
event_claims.quote_text:
Under the merger agreement, the Company and its affiliates and their respective representatives may not:

•

solicit, initiate or knowingly encourage or facilitate the making or submission of an alternative proposal

event_claims.actor_label: the Company and its affiliates and their respective representatives
event_claims.description: Under the merger agreement, the Company and its affiliates and their representatives may not participate in discussions or negotiations regarding an alternative proposal or furnish nonpublic information in connection with an alternative proposal, except to notify the person of the non-solicitation provisions.
event_claims.quote_text: participate in any discussions or negotiations regarding an alternative proposal with, or furnish any nonpublic information regarding the Company or its subsidiaries in connection with an alternative proposal to, any person that has made, or is considering making an alternative proposal (except to notify such person as to the non-solicitation provisions of the merger agreement)

event_claims.actor_label: the Company and its affiliates and their respective representatives
event_claims.description: Under the merger agreement, the Company and its affiliates and their representatives may not enter into an agreement relating to or providing for an alternative proposal, except permitted confidentiality agreements.
event_claims.quote_text: enter into any letter of intent, agreement in principle, merger or acquisition agreement or any other agreement relating to or providing for any alternative proposal (except for confidentiality agreements permitted under the merger agreement).

event_claims.description: Before the stockholder vote, if the Company receives an unsolicited alternative proposal from a third party that the board judges is or could lead to a superior proposal, the Company may take permitted actions.
event_claims.quote_text: prior to obtaining the required vote of the Company’s stockholders to adopt the merger agreement, the Company receives an unsolicited alternative proposal from a third party that in the good faith judgment of the board, after consultation with outside legal and financial advisors, constitutes, or could reasonably be expected to result in, a “superior proposal”

event_claims.description: The Company may provide nonpublic information to the third party after entering into a confidentiality agreement acceptable under the merger agreement.
event_claims.quote_text: provide nonpublic information to the third party after entering into a confidentiality agreement acceptable under the terms of the merger agreement

event_claims.description: The Company may engage in discussions or negotiations with a third party regarding the alternative proposal.
event_claims.quote_text: engage in discussions or negotiations with a third party with respect to the alternative proposal

event_claims.description: Before the board changes its recommendation or the Company terminates to accept a superior proposal, the Company must provide Parent written notice of the board's intention and describe the superior proposal terms.
event_claims.quote_text: provide Parent written notice of the board’s intention to make a change in recommendation or to terminate the merger agreement with a description of the terms of the superior proposal

event_claims.description: Before the board changes its recommendation or the Company terminates to accept a superior proposal, the Company must provide Parent its reasons in writing.
event_claims.quote_text: provide to Parent in writing its reasons for making the change in recommendation or for terminating the merger agreement

event_claims.description: Parent must be given three business days to propose changes to the merger agreement terms, with extensions for material changes in the superior proposal, and the Company must negotiate with Parent in good faith during that period.
event_claims.quote_text: provide Parent with three business days to propose changes to the terms of the merger agreement (which will be extended for additional two business day periods for any material change in the superior proposal), and during this period negotiate with Parent in good faith to make adjustments to the terms and conditions of the merger agreement

event_claims.description: After the matching-right period, the board must determine that the superior proposal remains superior after taking into account Parent's proposed revisions.
event_claims.quote_text: Following the end of such three business day period (and any extension thereto), after taking into account any revisions to the terms and conditions of the merger agreement proposed by Parent, the board must determine that the superior proposal remains superior to the merger proposal.

event_claims.description: If the Company terminates the merger agreement to enter into an agreement with a third party regarding a superior proposal, or if the board changes recommendation in response to a superior proposal and Parent terminates, the Company must pay Parent a $255 million termination fee.
event_claims.quote_text: If the Company terminates the merger agreement to enter into an agreement with a third party regarding a superior proposal or the board changes its recommendation in response to a superior proposal and the agreement is terminated by Parent, then the Company must pay a termination fee of $255 million to Parent

event_claims.description: The Company may terminate the merger agreement before the Company stockholder meeting to accept a superior proposal if it pays the termination fee and promptly enters into a definitive agreement for that proposal.
event_claims.quote_text:
at any time prior to the Company stockholder meeting to accept a “superior proposal” (defined below under The Merger Agreement — Other Covenants and Agreements — No Solicitation ) so

long as (i) prior to or substantially concurrent with such termination, the Company has paid a termination fee of $255 million to Parent and (ii) promptly after such termination, the Company enters into a definitive agreement with respect to such superior proposal

event_claims.description: Parent may terminate the merger agreement if the board makes a change of recommendation before the stockholder meeting to adopt the merger agreement.
event_claims.quote_text: if the board takes an action that constitutes a change of recommendation (as defined below under The Merger Agreement — Other Covenants and Agreements — No Solicitation ) prior to the stockholder meeting to adopt the merger agreement

event_claims.description: The Company will pay Parent a $255 million termination fee if it terminates before stockholder approval in response to a superior proposal and promptly enters into a definitive agreement for that proposal.
event_claims.quote_text: the Company has terminated the merger agreement before stockholder approval of the merger proposal is obtained in response to a superior proposal and promptly after such termination the Company enters into a definitive agreement with respect to such superior proposal

event_claims.description: The Company will pay Parent a $255 million termination fee if Parent terminates due to a change of recommendation.
event_claims.quote_text: Parent has terminated the merger agreement in the event of a change of recommendation
